svg.text: Step 1: Why Elevate to D+1 Dimensions?
Using 3 symmetric coordinates to turn simplex lookup into sorting (D = 2 example)

g.text: Simplex grids need fewer vertices (D+1 instead of 2ⁿ),
but standard (x, y) coordinates don't align with them.
We need a coordinate system where
simplex lookup is as simple as floor() for rectangles.
That coordinate system is the permutohedral lattice.
6 orderings of 3 values = 6 simplices per cell
Why Must They Sum to Zero?
The constraint a + b + c = 0 has three essential consequences
Same information as (x, y), just rearranged
every coordinate plays the same role
Simplex vertices = permutations of each other
a = b, b = c, a = c
Sorting D+1 values: O(D log D)
